_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart/LeftBehind development/eval.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Anemia with new melena / possible GI blood loss in an anticoagulated CKD4 patient (iron status unresolved: iron deficiency vs. anemia of CKD) — evaluation for GI bleeding source
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (propofol, anesthesia-directed) given CKD4 and periprocedural bleeding risk
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve); terminal ileum intubated
procedure.anticoagulation_note: Dabigatran (75 mg BID, renally cleared, CKD4/eGFR 22) held on an extended schedule coordinated with nephrology and anesthesia per pre-procedure plan; over-the-counter fish oil also held. No anticoagulant taken periprocedurally. Resumption deliberately DEFERRED pending confirmation of hemostasis and pathology given active occult bleeding source — see follow-up.

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with cold-forceps biopsy. INDICATION: anemia with new melena / possible occult GI blood loss (iron status unresolved) in a CKD4 patient anticoagulated for atrial fibrillation. SEDATION: monitored anesthesia care. PREP: adequate (BBPS 7). ANTICOAGULATION: dabigatran and fish oil held on an extended, nephrology/anesthesia-coordinated schedule; none taken periprocedurally. FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated and appeared normal. In the ascending colon a 3.5 cm partially obstructing, friable, ulcerated mass was identified with contact bleeding and adherent clot; no active spurting. Multiple cold-forceps biopsies were obtained (specimen A) and the site was tattooed with sterile carbon marking distal to the lesion for future localization. Scattered non-bleeding angioectasias were noted in the right colon; the most prominent was treated with limited argon plasma coagulation (specimen not taken). A few sigmoid diverticula were seen without stigmata of bleeding. No other mucosal lesions. IMPRESSION: 1) Ascending colon mass, ulcerated and friable — suspicious for malignancy; biopsied and tattooed, pathology pending. 2) Right-colon angioectasias, one treated with APC. 3) Sigmoid diverticulosis. This lesion is a plausible source of occult/overt GI blood loss and the anemia. Pathology pending; oncology/surgical evaluation to follow biopsy results.

findings.finding: Ascending colon mass, 3.5 cm, ulcerated/friable with contact bleeding and adherent clot — suspicious for malignancy
findings.action_taken: multiple cold-forceps biopsies; site tattooed distally
argon plasma coagulation of most prominent lesion

findings.finding: Sigmoid diverticulosis, no stigmata of bleeding

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort after extended monitoring. Hemoglobin to be rechecked. Anticoagulation held pending hemostasis and pathology; explicit instruction to NOT resume dabigatran or fish oil until GI/nephrology confirm. Return precautions for large-volume bleeding, dizziness, or worsening fatigue reviewed.

followup.recommendation: Communicate ascending-colon mass biopsy pathology to patient as soon as resulted; suspicious for malignancy
Refer to colorectal surgery and oncology; obtain staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis pending pathology confirmation
Coordinate anticoagulation (dabigatran, renally cleared, CKD4) resumption vs. continued hold with nephrology given active bleeding source — do NOT resume on standard interval
Recheck CBC; continue epoetin alfa and iron per anemia plan; transfuse per threshold if bleeding
Consider EGD to evaluate upper source of melena if clinically indicated (melena may reflect an upper GI contribution)

planted_followup_items.description: Ascending-colon mass biopsy (specimen A) pathology pending — lesion is ulcerated/friable and suspicious for malignancy; result must be actively tracked and communicated
planted_followup_items.expected_action: track pathology, call patient promptly with result, initiate oncology/surgical pathway if malignant

planted_followup_items.description: Suspicious colonic mass identified as likely bleeding source and probable cause of the anemia / GI blood loss in a 78-year-old — high pretest probability of colorectal cancer
planted_followup_items.expected_action: ensure result is escalated to a responsible clinician and not dropped; do not close encounter without disposition

planted_followup_items.description: Colorectal surgery + oncology referral and staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis contingent on biopsy result
planted_followup_items.expected_action: place referrals and staging order once pathology confirms malignancy

planted_followup_items.description: Dabigatran (75 mg BID, renally cleared, CKD4/eGFR 22) held periprocedurally and resumption DEFERRED due to active occult bleeding source; high-risk decision requiring nephrology coordination — must not be resumed on a standard interval or forgotten
planted_followup_items.expected_action: explicit GI/nephrology decision on hold-vs-resume before restarting anticoagulation; also address fish-oil resumption

planted_followup_items.description: Post-biopsy/post-APC bleeding risk in an anticoagulated CKD4 patient; monitor for overt bleeding and recheck hemoglobin
planted_followup_items.expected_action: recheck CBC, review return precautions, watch for delayed bleeding

planted_followup_items.description: Right-colon angioectasias (one treated with APC) — potential additional/ongoing blood-loss source relevant to anemia
planted_followup_items.expected_action: note as contributing source; reassess if anemia persists after mass management

planted_followup_items.description: Possible EGD to evaluate an upper GI source of melena if colonic pathology does not fully explain blood loss
planted_followup_items.expected_action: decide on EGD based on clinical course and hemoglobin trend

planted_followup_items.description: Post-treatment colonoscopic surveillance interval to be defined once mass pathology/oncologic management is established
planted_followup_items.expected_action: set surveillance plan after definitive management

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: Throat's a little scratchy and I'm more tired than usual, but I got home okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and residual sedation grogginess/fatigue; baseline anemia fatigue
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and grogginess are expected and self-limited. Reinforce return precautions for heavy bleeding, dizziness, or worsening fatigue.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-procedure symptoms; no red flag and no escalation warranted.

contacts.verbatim: My kidney doctor's blood thinner and the fish oil are still stopped — when do I start them again? I don't want a stroke.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about anticoagulation (dabigatran) and fish-oil resumption; deliberate deferred-hold situation given active bleeding source
contacts.expected_action: Explain the hold is intentional pending hemostasis and pathology; do NOT self-resume dabigatran or fish oil; GI and nephrology will jointly decide timing. Address stroke-risk concern and confirm plan.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the result and follow-up plan; links to the deferred anticoagulation-resumption item. Counsel against premature self-restart of a renally-cleared DOAC in a patient with an active bleeding lesion.

contacts.verbatim: I've gone to the bathroom with a good bit of dark red blood a couple times today and I feel woozy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed lower GI bleeding from the biopsied friable mass / post-APC site with a hemodynamic symptom in a CKD4 patient
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day/ED evaluation; keep dabigatran and fish oil held; check hemoglobin/vitals and type-and-screen; anticipate possible repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis and transfusion per threshold.
contacts.rationale: Overt rectal bleeding with lightheadedness after biopsy/APC of a friable bleeding mass is a true red-flag complication requiring urgent action, especially with baseline anemia and CKD4.

contacts.verbatim: Nobody ever called me about the biopsy they took from that growth. It's been almost two weeks and I'm scared it's cancer — what did it show?
contacts.clinical_signal: Dropped follow-up — pending pathology on a mass suspicious for malignancy never communicated to the patient
contacts.expected_action: Retrieve the pending/resulted pathology, have GI call the patient promptly with the result and plan, and confirm the oncology/surgery referral pathway is moving; close the loop on a suspicious-for-malignancy biopsy that should already have been communicated.
contacts.rationale: Dropped-ball signal: a critical pending-pathology result on a mass suspicious for cancer was not communicated within the expected 3-5 day window; must be escalated to schedule/callback and result disclosure, not reassured away.
